Title: VvHDZ28 positively regulate salicylic acid biosynthesis during seed abortion in Thompson Seedless

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: promotes biosynthesis/signaling leading to SA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling; associated with programmed cell death and embryo abortion in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript levels in Thompson Seedless seeds than in Pinot Noir across seed developmental stages examined
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in Thompson Seedless than Pinot Noir seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 20, 30, 40, 50 DAF; exogenous SA treatment did not significantly alter EDS1 expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; endogenous SA quantification; ChIP-qPCR for H3K27me3; transgenic tomato overexpression of upstream regulator VvHDZ28 followed by expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Not a catalytic enzyme in this paper; functions upstream to promote SA biosynthesis/signaling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not enzymatically defined
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher VvEDS1 expression is associated with elevated SA accumulation and seed abortion in Thompson Seedless
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvEDS1 transcripts were higher in Thompson Seedless seeds than in Pinot Noir. 2. Elevated VvEDS1 expression correlated with higher endogenous salicylic acid levels. 3. H3K27me3 enrichment at the VvEDS1 promoter in Pinot Noir correlated with lower expression. 4. VvHDZ28 directly activated the VvEDS1 promoter. 5. Overexpression of VvHDZ28 in tomato increased EDS homolog expression and SA content.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native VvEDS1 promoter for reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering seedlessness via SA pathway modulation, but may cause reproductive defects through excessive SA
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter-proximal H3K27me3 enrichment was significantly higher in Pinot Noir and correlated with reduced expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although named as SA biosynthesis-related, the paper presents VvEDS1 as an upstream regulator rather than a demonstrated catalytic enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcriptional regulator associated with SA biosynthesis control
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling; associated with programmed cell death and embryo abortion in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript levels in Thompson Seedless seeds than in Pinot Noir
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in Thompson Seedless than Pinot Noir seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 20, 30, 40, 50 DAF; exogenous SA treatment did not significantly alter SARD1 expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; endogenous SA quantification; ChIP-qPCR for H3K27me3; promoter activation assays by VvHDZ28; transgenic tomato overexpression of VvHDZ28 followed by expression analysis of SARD homologs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Not a catalytic enzyme in this paper; acts as positive regulator of SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not enzymatically defined
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher VvSARD1 expression is associated with elevated SA accumulation and seed abortion in Thompson Seedless
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvSARD1 transcripts were higher in Thompson Seedless seeds than in Pinot Noir. 2. Elevated VvSARD1 expression correlated with higher endogenous salicylic acid levels. 3. H3K27me3 enrichment at the VvSARD1 promoter in Pinot Noir correlated with reduced expression. 4. VvHDZ28 activated the VvSARD1 promoter in reporter assays, but direct binding was not supported by yeast one-hybrid. 5. Overexpression of VvHDZ28 in tomato increased SARD homolog expression and SA content.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native VvSARD1 promoter for reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be leveraged indirectly to modulate SA-linked seedlessness, with caution due to fertility defects
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter-proximal H3K27me3 enrichment was significantly higher in Pinot Noir and correlated with reduced expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SARD1 is biologically a regulator, but included here because the paper repeatedly groups it with SA biosynthesis-related core genes; VvHDZ28 appears to regulate it indirectly.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HD-Zip I family homeodomain-leucine zipper protein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling; associated with programmed cell death and embryo abortion/seedlessness in this study
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds in grape; inflorescences/flowers in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in Thompson Seedless than Pinot Noir at all four seed developmental stages; detected in tomato leaves, roots, flowers, shoots and fruits in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in Thompson Seedless seeds than in Pinot Noir seeds
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 20–50 DAF; promoter induced by salicylic acid treatment
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates VvEDS1 directly by promoter binding; activates VvSARD1 promoter activity but not via confirmed direct binding in yeast one-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; feedback induction by SA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback with salicylic acid, as SA induces VvHDZ28 promoter and VvHDZ28 elevates SA via EDS1/SARD1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; SA treatment with pHDZ28::GUS assay; dual-luciferase assay; GUS reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; transgenic tomato overexpression; hormone quantification; floral/fruit phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes salicylic acid accumulation and causes seedless/parthenocarpic fruit phenotypes with reproductive defects when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator linking SA biosynthesis to seed abortion and seedlessness
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvHDZ28 is highly expressed in Thompson Seedless seeds relative to Pinot Noir. 2. VvHDZ28 promoter is induced by salicylic acid. 3. VvHDZ28 activates VvEDS1 and VvSARD1 promoters, but direct DNA binding was verified only for VvEDS1. 4. Overexpression in tomato increases SA content and EDS/SARD homolog expression. 5. Overexpression causes abnormal flowers, reduced fertility, and parthenocarpic/seedless fruits.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Abnormal flower development, loss of stamens, reduced fertility, parthenocarpy, low seed set, fruit shriveling in many flowers
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for breeding or engineering seedlessness, but strong pleiotropic reproductive defects necessitate careful dosage, tissue-specific expression, or fine-tuned editing
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter in tomato overexpression; native VvHDZ28 promoter used in SA-induction GUS assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes VvHDZ28 upregulates VvSARD1 indirectly while directly binding the VvEDS1 promoter TAATTA cis-element.
